heroTitle: The exam has a shape. Learn the shape.
heroSub: Every Reinforcement Learning final follows the same template: four questions spanning planning (MDPs & dynamic programming), model-free learning, function approximation & policy gradient, and bandits. Built from your course's 13 lectures, 12 recitations, the RL Foundations book, and all past exams (2018–2023) — translated from Hebrew, tagged, and drillable.
examFormatLine: 3 hours · one A4 double-sided aid sheet + calculator · 4 questions · ~100 points

slotRoles.Planning: known-model dynamic programming · MDPs, Bellman & contraction, value/policy iteration, LQR
slotRoles.Learning: model-free from samples · Monte Carlo, TD(0)/TD(λ), SARSA, Q-learning, convergence
slotRoles.Approximation: scaling up · function approximation, policy gradient theorem, actor-critic
slotRoles.Bandits: exploration & regret · multi-armed bandits, UCB, best-arm identification

askSuggestions: why is the Bellman optimality operator a γ-contraction?
Q-learning convergence and the Robbins-Monro conditions
TD(0) vs Monte Carlo: bias, variance, and bootstrapping
state the policy gradient theorem and the REINFORCE estimator
UCB regret bound for multi-armed bandits
value iteration vs policy iteration: convergence and cost
